stimulus.content: The Andes Mountains run along the western coast of South America. These tall mountains made it very hard for people on the coast to trade with people living on the plains to the east. Ancient civilizations like the Inca built special roads and bridges to cross the mountains.

stem: How did the Andes Mountains affect trade and civilization in South America?

choices.text: They created a barrier that made east-west trade difficult
They provided a flat and easy path for trade wagons
They blocked all rivers from flowing to the ocean
They had no effect on how civilizations developed

answer_explanation: The Andes acted as a natural barrier between the coast and the interior, making trade difficult. The Inca built roads and bridges to overcome this challenge.
